channel.title: math.SP updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: math.SP updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Revisiting the Weak Coupling Phenomenon for Two-Dimensional Schr\"odinger Operators
item.description: arXiv:2604.19284v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study the existence of negative eigenvalues for two-dimensional Schr\"odinger operators with real-valued potentials in the weak coupling regime. In his pioneering paper [Simon 1976] from half a century ago, Simon was the first to describe the unique negative eigenvalue emerging from the threshold of the essential spectrum of one- and two-dimensional Schr\"odinger operators. The aim of this paper is to extend Simon's results in two dimensions to a broader class of potentials, allowing for both stronger singularities and slower decay at infinity, at the cost of losing uniqueness of weakly coupled eigenvalues.
item.dc:creator: Jussi Behrndt, Petr Siegl, Nicolas Weber

item.title: Some New Results on Energy of Graphs with Self Loops
item.description: arXiv:2604.18651v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: The graph $G_\sigma$ is obtained from graph $G$ by attaching self loops on $\sigma$ vertices. The energy $ E(G_\sigma)$ of the graph $G_\sigma$ with order $n$ and eigenvalues $\lambda_1,\lambda_2,\dots,\lambda_n$ is defined as $ E(G_\sigma)= \displaystyle \sum_{i=1}^n\left|\lambda_i-\dfrac{\sigma}{n}\right| $. It has been proved that if $\sigma=0\; or\; n$ then $ E(G)=E(G_\sigma) $. The obvious question arise: Are there any graph such that $E(G)=E(G_\sigma)$ and 0$<\sigma<n$? We have found an affirmative answer of this question and contributed a graph family which satisfies this property.
item.dc:creator: Kalpesh M. Popat, Kunal R. Shingala

item.title: The Steklov spectrum of convex polygonal domains II: investigating spectral determination
item.description: arXiv:2604.18977v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: The extent to which the geometry of an object is determined by some associated spectral data is a longstanding problem. We investigate this problem in the context of the Steklov spectrum, focusing on convex polygons. We prove that almost all triangles are uniquely determined by their Steklov spectra within the class of all triangles; further results depending on the types of angles in the triangles are given. We examine three special classes of convex quadrilaterals--rectangles, parallelograms, and kites--and obtain results ranging from unique spectral determination to determination up to three possibilities. For regular $n$-gons, we are again able to prove spectral determination within certain classes of polygons. More generally, we investigate the extent to which the Steklov spectrum distinguishes convex polygons from simply-connected domains with smooth boundary; that is, does the Steklov spectrum detect corners? We prove that triangles and quadrilaterals are spectrally distinguished from such smoothly bounded domains; moreover, we show that having the same Steklov spectrum as such a domain imposes substantial restrictions on the edge lengths of higher-order $n$-gons. Throughout, our main tool is the characteristic polynomial developed in works by Stanislav Krymski, Michael Levitin, Leonid Parnovski, Iosif Polterovich, and David A. Sher.
item.dc:creator: Emily B. Dryden, Carolyn Gordon, Javier Moreno, Julie Rowlett, Carlos Villegas-Blas

item.title: Flux effects on Magnetic Laplace and Steklov eigenvalues in the exterior of a disk
item.description: arXiv:2508.18119v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We derive a three-term asymptotic expansion for the lowest eigenvalue of the magnetic Laplace and Steklov operators in the exterior of the unit disk in the strong magnetic field limit. This improves recent results of Helffer-Nicoleau (2025) based on special function asymptotics, and extends earlier works by Fournais-Helffer (2006), Kachmar (2006), and R. Fahs, L. Treust, N. Raymond, S. V\~u Ng\d{o}c (2024). Notably, our analysis reveals how the third term encodes the dependence on the magnetic flux. Finally, we investigate the weak magnetic field limit and establish the flux dependence in the asymptotics of Kachmar-Lotoreichik-Sundqvist (2025).
item.dc:creator: Bernard Helffer, Ayman Kachmar, Fran\c{c}ois Nicoleau

item.title: Factorization for the matrix-valued general Jacobi system on the full-line lattice
item.description: arXiv:2511.18229v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: The Jacobi system with matrix-valued coefficients and with the spectral parameter depending on a matrix-valued weight factor is considered on the full-line lattice. The scattering from the full-line lattice is expressed in terms of the scattering from the fragments of the whole lattice by developing a factorization formula for the corresponding transition matrices. In particular, the matrix-valued transmission and reflection coefficients for the full-line lattice are explicitly expressed in terms of the scattering coefficients for the left and right lattice fragments. Since the matrix-valued scattering coefficients are easier to determine for the fragments than for the full-line lattice, the factorization formula presented provides a method to determine the scattering coefficients for full-line lattices. The theory presented is illustrated with various explicit examples, including an example demonstrating that the matrix-valued left transmission coefficient in general is not equal to the matrix-valued right transmission coefficient for a lattice.
item.dc:creator: Tuncay Aktosun, Abdon E. Choque-Rivero, Vassilis G. Papanicolaou, Mehmet Unlu, Ricardo Weder

item.title: Ergodicity in discrete-time quantum walks
item.description: arXiv:2603.16837v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: We undertake a detailed analysis of ergodicity for homogeneous discrete-time quantum walks on the integer lattice. The most significant result of our paper holds in dimension one, and gives a complete equivalence between the absolutely continuous spectrum of the unitary operator encoding the walk, and the equidistribution of its dynamics in position space, which appears for the first time in the context of large-volume quantum ergodicity. In higher dimensions, we give a criterion for full and partial ergodicity in terms of a finer property of the spectrum which we dub ``No Repeating Graphs'', and we distinguish how strongly the equidistribution is taking place (weak convergence vs total variation). Many examples are included to illustrate the criterion and to distinguish between the types of ergodicity.
item.dc:creator: Kiran Kumar, Mostafa Sabri
